title: Extension and Development Corps (EDC) history — GFRAS world-wide extension study, Iran country page
source_org: Global Forum for Rural Advisory Services (GFRAS)
time_coverage: 1964-1979 (plus one modern-era 2003 comparison figure, out of core scope but retained for context)
notes: This is the only source found in this pass with concrete, dated quantitative detail specifically for the third White Revolution corps (Extension/Development/Reconstruction Corps, Sepah-e Tarvij va Abadani), which is generally the least-documented of the three corps in English-language sources. Confirms 1964 founding, 18-month service structure, and a 1965 fertilizer-adoption figure (90,000 tons) attributed to EDC intervention. Underlying primary source is 1967 CENTO Conference on Agricultural Extension papers (Jaffari, P. and Rassi, J.) which were not independently located/downloaded in this pass -- CENTO proceedings are not readily available online; flagged as a follow-up opportunity. The 2003 staff-count figures in the same source are modern-era and out of the pre-1990 gap this hunt targets, but kept for context since they were on the same page and provide a useful before/after anchor for extension-service scale.
